title: Spec-decode coverage + decode tok/s across the fleet
subtitle: 2x R9700 TP=2, --speculative-attention-mode decode. Bars = AWQ production ships measured SHORT-DEPTH on a 256K-capable server (NOT at 256K decode depth — see DEPTH CAVEAT). FP8-target spec measured separately 2026-05-31 (see fp8-comparison.json)
note: ⚠ DEPTH CAVEAT (2026-06-15): every spec_toks below is SHORT-DEPTH on a 256K-CAPABLE server, NOT decode at 256K depth. At TRUE 256K depth on real content, spec COLLAPSES: Coder-30B EAGLE3 AWQ 107 (short) -> 0.8 @244K (accept 6.12->1.75) = ~15x slower than no-spec @244K (12.3); Qwen3.6-35B DFlash AWQ ~1.2 @240K (accept ~1.4) vs the '80' here. Universal across arch+draft (draft accept craters at depth + draft attends the full KV every micro-step). These bars measure server CAPABILITY, not 256K-depth throughput; for the 256K mandate no-spec is the path, spec is <=~32-64K only. See benchmarks/spec-at-depth-collapse-2026-06-15.md. --- Single-bar = best production spec-decode decode tok/s (target + draft + --speculative-attention-mode decode @ TP2). speedup x is over the family's ~22 tok/s no-spec baseline. 'untested' = a viable draft path exists but unbenched; 'blocked' = no working draft yet, with the blocker. As blocked/untested models get fixed, flip status->working + fill spec_toks and regenerate — no model should sit at a bare N/A. Data sourced from the README spec-decode summary + coverage map. FP8-TARGET note (2026-05-31, post-iommu=pt): these bars are AWQ-target production numbers (short-ctx spec works without iommu=pt, so they stand). FP8 targets differ: Coder-30B-FP8+EAGLE3 = ~86 tok/s coherent (256K served, tiny 361MB draft); Qwen3.6-35B-FP8+DFlash = ~45 tok/s coherent AT 256K (accept ~3.9) via --chunked-prefill 2048 — the prior 'OOMs@256K' was a prefill-transient, not a draft wall (KV pool 782K was never the limit). So BOTH A3B-MoE FP8 models do FP8+draft@256K (Coder-30B EAGLE3 ~86, Qwen3.6-35B DFlash ~45). The 80 here is the AWQ ship (faster — Marlin-class int4 verify); FP8 trails AWQ on absolute tok/s but both reach 256K.

models.name: Qwen3.5-28B-A3B-REAP
models.reason: TESTED 2026-06-14: parent Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-DFlash transfer loads, coherent, 4/4 caps (basic+thinking+vision+video), accepts (len 2-8 workload-dep). Authoritative server-log sustained gen-throughput ~52 tok/s short-ctx vs ~60 no-spec = net-negative-to-neutral; block-diffusion draft+verify overhead cancels the acceptance benefit, and decode collapses deep (<=~2 tok/s by ~240K, DeltaNet+DFlash wall). No useful speedup; corroborates 3090's 'DFlash moot'. NOTE: client-tool TPOT under-measures spec ~2x (SSE buffering) - use server-log.

models.name: Qwen3.5-27B / Qwen3.6-27B
models.reason: TESTED EAGLE3 2026-06-14 (Qwen3.6-27B-EAGLE3-specdrift, LlamaForCausalLMEagle3 1.25GB): now LOADS+runs+coherent on the DeltaNet-VL target after patch 055 (LlamaConfig decoupled-head-dim validator + qwen3_vl EAGLE3 capture-layer marking — both were hard load blockers). Accepts WELL (accept len 4.0, topk4/steps3/draft8) but NET-NEGATIVE: server-log gen throughput ~18 tok/s vs ~25 no-spec. ROOT CAUSE: spec step is GPU-COMPUTE-bound, not dispatch-bound — the dense 27B is 75% DeltaNet (3:1 linear/full) and DeltaNet's recurrent verify is SEQUENTIAL in num-draft-tokens (verifying 8 tree tokens ~= 8 serial state updates), so the step costs ~5.5x a plain decode while yielding only 4x tokens. cuda-graph gives ZERO help (18.3 ON vs 18.1 OFF) confirming compute-bound not launch-bound. Deeper trees make verify MORE expensive -> no tuning fixes it. Extends 3090's 'DFlash moot on DeltaNet' to EAGLE3, isolating the wall to recurrent verify cost (not draft quality). DFlash also OOMs/net-neg; FP8 won't fit 2x32GB. The only lever would be a DeltaNet-aware chunked/parallel verify kernel (deep kernel work). Patch 055 still unblocks EAGLE3 LOADING for the whole qwen3_5/qwen3_vl VL family (helps any pure-attention member, e.g. it's a prerequisite for Qwen3-VL-32B EAGLE3 once #17935 is past).

models.name: gemma-4-31B / 26B
models.reason: no DFLASH hook; FROZEN_KV_MTP needs transformers 5.8 + SGLang fix; base EAGLE3 draft accept only 1.34 (needs it-matched draft)

models.name: Qwen3-VL-32B
models.reason: BLOCKER SHIFTED 2026-06-14: the SGLang-side #17935 gap is gone — qwen3_vl.get_embed_and_head is present AND patch 055 fixes the EAGLE3 capture-layer marking, so the VL EAGLE3 path now loads/engages. Remaining gap = NO off-the-shelf draft: taobao-mnn/Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct-Eagle3 is 2B (wrong geometry, won't transfer to 32B); RedHatAI/Qwen3-32B-speculator.eagle3 targets the TEXT 32B not VL. Needs a 32B-matched EAGLE3 draft trained via SpecForge (~27 H20-GPU-hrs). HIGH VALUE: Qwen3-VL-32B is PURE DENSE (head_dim 128, no DeltaNet, no MoE) so it has NO sequential-verify wall — EAGLE3 would actually WIN here like it does on Coder-30B (2.3x), unlike the DeltaNet 27B. The single best spec-decode target left on the fleet; gated only on training the draft.
